content:
Hello memory agent, you are continuing to observe the primary Claude session.

<observed_from_primary_session>
  <user_request>檢視ok了，幫我判斷是否需要整合至Skill</user_request>
  <requested_at>2026-06-16</requested_at>
</observed_from_primary_session>

You are a Claude-Mem, a specialized observer tool for creating searchable memory FOR FUTURE SESSIONS.

CRITICAL: Record what was LEARNED/BUILT/FIXED/DEPLOYED/CONFIGURED, not what you (the observer) are doing.

You do not have access to tools. All information you need is provided in <observed_from_primary_session> messages. Create observations from what you observe - no investigation needed.

Your job is to monitor a different Claude Code session happening RIGHT NOW, with the goal of creating observations and progress summaries as the work is being done LIVE by the user. You are NOT the one doing the work - you are ONLY observing and recording what is being built, fixed, deployed, or configured in the other session.

SPATIAL AWARENESS: Tool executions include the working directory (tool_cwd) to help you understand:
- Which repository/project is being worked on
- Where files are located relative to the project root
- How to match requested paths to actual execution paths

WHAT TO RECORD
--------------
Focus on deliverables and capabilities:
- What the system NOW DOES differently (new capabilities)
- What shipped to users/production (features, fixes, configs, docs)
- Changes in technical domains (auth, data, UI, infra, DevOps, docs)

Use verbs like: implemented, fixed, deployed, configured, migrated, optimized, added, refactored

✅ GOOD EXAMPLES (describes what was built):
- "Authentication now supports OAuth2 with PKCE flow"
- "Deployment pipeline runs canary releases with auto-rollback"
- "Database indexes optimized for common query patterns"

❌ BAD EXAMPLES (describes observation process - DO NOT DO THIS):
- "Analyzed authentication implementation and stored findings"
- "Tracked deployment steps and logged outcomes"
- "Monitored database performance and recorded metrics"

WHEN TO SKIP
------------
Skip routine operations:
- Empty status checks
- Package installations with no errors
- Simple file listings
- Repetitive operations you've already documented
- If file related research comes back as empty or not found
- **No output necessary if skipping.**

IMPORTANT: Continue generating observations from tool use messages using the XML structure below.

OUTPUT FORMAT
-------------
Output observations using this XML structure:

```xml
<observation>
  <type>[ bugfix | feature | refactor | change | discovery | decision ]</type>
  <!--
    **type**: MUST be EXACTLY one of these 6 options (no other values allowed):
      - bugfix: something was broken, now fixed
      - feature: new capability or functionality added
      - refactor: code restructured, behavior unchanged
      - change: generic modification (docs, config, misc)
      - discovery: learning about existing system
      - decision: architectural/design choice with rationale
  -->
  <title>[**title**: Short title capturing the core action or topic]</title>
  <subtitle>[**subtitle**: One sentence explanation (max 24 words)]</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
    <fact>[Concise, self-contained statement]</fact>
  </facts>
  <!--
    **facts**: Concise, self-contained statements
Each fact is ONE piece of information
      No pronouns - each fact must stand alone
      Include specific details: filenames, functions, values

**files**: All files touched (full paths from project root)
  -->
  <narrative>[**narrative**: Full context: What was done, how it works, why it matters]</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
    <concept>[knowledge-type-category]</concept>
  </concepts>
  <!--
    **concepts**: 2-5 knowledge-type categories. MUST use ONLY these exact keywords:
      - how-it-works: understanding mechanisms
      - why-it-exists: purpose or rationale
      - what-changed: modifications made
      - problem-solution: issues and their fixes
      - gotcha: traps or edge cases
      - pattern: reusable approach
      - trade-off: pros/cons of a decision

    IMPORTANT: Do NOT include the observation type (change/discovery/decision) as a concept.
    Types and concepts are separate dimensions.
  -->
  <files_read>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_read>
  <files_modified>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
    <file>[path/to/file]</file>
  </files_modified>
</observation>
```


IMPORTANT! DO NOT do any work right now other than generating this OBSERVATIONS from tool use messages - and remember that you are a memory agent designed to summarize a DIFFERENT claude code session, not this one.

Never reference yourself or your own actions. Do not output anything other than the observation content formatted in the XML structure above. All other output is ignored by the system, and the system has been designed to be smart about token usage. Please spend your tokens wisely on useful observations.

Remember that we record these observations as a way of helping us stay on track with our progress, and to help us keep important decisions and changes at the forefront of our minds! :) Thank you so much for your help!

MEMORY PROCESSING CONTINUED
===========================

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content:
<observed_from_primary_session>
  <what_happened>Read</what_happened>
  <occurred_at>2026-06-16T05:34:49.644Z</occurred_at>
  <working_directory>G:\Cocos_Project\uk_pirates_queen\assets\game\Text\logo</working_directory>
  <parameters>"{\"file_path\":\"C:\\\\Users\\\\jiunchiwang\\\\.claude\\\\skills\\\\uk-slot-multilang-sync\\\\SKILL.md\"}"</parameters>
  <outcome>"{\"type\":\"text\",\"file\":{\"filePath\":\"C:\\\\Users\\\\jiunchiwang\\\\.claude\\\\skills\\\\uk-slot-multilang-sync\\\\SKILL.md\",\"content\":\"---\\nname: uk-slot-multilang-sync\\ndescription: >-\\n  把規格 xlsx 的多國語言文字批次同步進 uk 線上老虎機框架各語系的\\n  gameStrings.xml。當使用者要在老虎機專案新增/更新多國語言字串、提到\\n  「gameStrings.xml」「多國語言」「多語系文字」「i18n 字串」「把 xlsx 的翻譯填進去」、\\n  或要為某個 Key 一次寫入多國(常見 26 國)文字時使用。僅適用線上部門 uk 框架\\n  (plist 格式 gameStrings.xml、每語系一目錄、欄標題括號內為語系代碼);\\n  商用部門或其他文字格式不適用。\\n---\\n\\n# uk 老虎機 多國語言同步\\n\\n把規格 xlsx「多國語言」工作表某幾列的各國文字,依指定 Key 批次 upsert 進\\n`assets/game/Text/<語系代碼>/gameStrings.xml`。一次處理所有語系,取代手動逐檔貼字。\\n\\n## 適用前提(先確認,不符就停)\\n\\n此 skill 假設 **uk 線上老虎機框架**的慣例:\\n\\n1. 每個語系一個目錄,目錄名 = 語系代碼(`tw`/`cn`/`en`/...),底下有 `gameStrings.xml`。\\n2. `gameStrings.xml` 是 plist,每個字串是一個標籤:`<KEY>文字</KEY>`,全部在 `<dict>...</dict>` 內。\\n3. xlsx「多國語言」工作表:**列 = Key、欄 = 語系**,語系欄標題的括號內就是代碼,\\n   例如「繁體中文(tw)zh-TW」→ `tw`。\\n\\n驗證一次:`ls assets/game/Text` 看是否一堆語系目錄、各有 `gameStrings.xml`;\\n打開一個確認是 `<KEY>文字</KEY>` 格式。不符合(例如商用部門用別種格式)→ **不要用此 skill**,改手動或另議。\\n\\n## 工作流\\n\\n### 1. 檢視 xlsx,確認語系欄與列號\\n\\n用 openpyxl 看「多國語言」工作表的第 1 列(欄標題)與要處理的資料列:\\n\\n```python\\nimport openpyxl\\nwb = openpyxl.load_workbook(\\\"規格.xlsx\\\", read_only=True, data_only=True)\\nws = wb[[s for s in wb.sheetnames if \\\"多國語言\\\" in s][0]]\\nfor i, row in enumerate(ws.iter_rows(min_row=1, max_row=60, values_only=True), 1):\\n    print(i, [ (c or \\\"\\\")[:20] if isinstance(c, str) else c for c in row[:5] ])\\n```\\n\\n### 2. 建對應表(由你 agent 建立,不是叫使用者手填)\\n\\n`string_keys.tsv` 是中繼產物,由 **你(agent)** 依使用者給的資訊建立/填寫。\\n使用者通常只會「動嘴」:說「把這幾個 Key 加進去」或「Key=X 在第 N 列」。你的工作:\\n\\n- 使用者給了 Key + 列號 → 直接落成 tsv 各一行。\\n- 使用者只給 Key 清單、不知道列號 → 你去 xlsx 比對文字幫他定位列號,再填。\\n- 只有單筆 → 臨時建一行的 tsv 也行(例如 `_claude_temp/string_keys.tsv`)。\\n\\n複製 `scripts/string_keys.tsv` 範本當起點,每行 `Key <Tab> 列號 <Tab> 備註`。\\n\\n🔴 **列號 = xlsx 左側的「實際 Excel 列號」,不是內部「編號」欄。** 編號欄常中途斷掉,拿來當識別會錯位。\\n\\n### 3. dry-run 預覽 + 語意核對(最重要)\\n\\n```bash\\npython <此skill>/scripts/sync_game_strings.py --xlsx 規格.xlsx --mapping _claude_temp/string_keys.tsv\\n```\\n\\ndry-run 只印不寫。**務必核對每個 Key 對到的列,語意是否正確** —— 把該列的 en/tw 文字\\n跟現有 XML 裡同 Key 的值比一比。實務上「列號填錯一格」很常見且 dry-run 數字看起來照樣正常\\n(例如 EXTRA_BET_DESC 本該對「OFF→ON 開啟選擇畫面」那列,卻填到「初始乘倍」那列),\\n唯一抓得到的方法就是人眼核對語意。報告會列出每語系「新增/更新/缺字」,缺翻譯的語系要回報補齊。\\n\\n### 4. 確認無誤才寫入\\n\\n```bash\\npython <此skill>/scripts/sync_game_strings.py --xlsx 規格.xlsx --mapping _claude_temp/string_keys.tsv --write\\n```\\n\\n行為:Key 已存在 → 覆蓋更新;不存在 → 插在 `</dict>` 前。保留各檔原本換行(CRLF/LF)與 UTF-8,\\n文字自動做 XML 跳脫(`& < >`)。寫入後用 `git diff` 抽查 2-3 個語系確認內容正確。\\n\\n## 腳本參數\\n\\n`scripts/sync_game_strings.py`(路徑全相對「當前工作目錄」,在專案根目錄執行):\\n\\n| 參數 | 預設 | 說明 |\\n|------|------|------|\\n| `--xlsx` | (必填) | 規格 xlsx 路徑 |\\n| `--mapping` | `./string_keys.tsv` | Key↔列號對應表 |\\n| `--text-dir` | `./assets/game/Text` | 語系目錄根 |\\n| `--sheet` | 自動偵測含「多國語言」者 | 工作表名(多個符合時需手動指定) |\\n| `--xml-name` | `gameStrings.xml` | 各語系內 XML 檔名 |\\n| `--write` | (關;預設 dry-run) | 加上才實際寫入 |\\n\\n需要 `openpyxl`(`pip install openpyxl`)。\\n\\n## 常見問題\\n\\n- **某語系報「找不到對應 gameStrings.xml」**:欄標題括號代碼與目錄名對不上 → 檢查 xlsx 標題或目錄。\\n- **缺字(∅)**:該列在某語系儲存格是空的 → 翻譯未提供,回報請對方補,腳本不會寫空字串。\\n- **git 跳 `CRLF will be replaced by LF`**:repo 換行設定行為,非腳本造成,磁碟檔仍為原換行。\\n- **新 Key 大量新增後**順序在 `</dict>` 前,屬正常;不影響讀取。\\n\",\"numLines\":97,\"startLine\":1,\"totalLines\":97}}"</outcome>
</observed_from_primary_session>

<observed_from_primary_session>
  <what_happened>Read</what_happened>
  <occurred_at>2026-06-16T05:34:49.644Z</occurred_at>
  <working_directory>G:\Cocos_Project\uk_pirates_queen\assets\game\Text\logo</working_directory>
  <parameters>"{\"file_path\":\"C:\\\\Users\\\\jiunchiwang\\\\.claude\\\\skills\\\\uk-slot-multilang-sync\\\\SKILL.md\"}"</parameters>
  <outcome>"{\"type\":\"text\",\"file\":{\"filePath\":\"C:\\\\Users\\\\jiunchiwang\\\\.claude\\\\skills\\\\uk-slot-multilang-sync\\\\SKILL.md\",\"content\":\"---\\nname: uk-slot-multilang-sync\\ndescription: >-\\n  把規格 xlsx 的多國語言文字批次同步進 uk 線上老虎機框架各語系的\\n  gameStrings.xml。當使用者要在老虎機專案新增/更新多國語言字串、提到\\n  「gameStrings.xml」「多國語言」「多語系文字」「i18n 字串」「把 xlsx 的翻譯填進去」、\\n  或要為某個 Key 一次寫入多國(常見 26 國)文字時使用。僅適用線上部門 uk 框架\\n  (plist 格式 gameStrings.xml、每語系一目錄、欄標題括號內為語系代碼);\\n  商用部門或其他文字格式不適用。\\n---\\n\\n# uk 老虎機 多國語言同步\\n\\n把規格 xlsx「多國語言」工作表某幾列的各國文字,依指定 Key 批次 upsert 進\\n`assets/game/Text/<語系代碼>/gameStrings.xml`。一次處理所有語系,取代手動逐檔貼字。\\n\\n## 適用前提(先確認,不符就停)\\n\\n此 skill 假設 **uk 線上老虎機框架**的慣例:\\n\\n1. 每個語系一個目錄,目錄名 = 語系代碼(`tw`/`cn`/`en`/...),底下有 `gameStrings.xml`。\\n2. `gameStrings.xml` 是 plist,每個字串是一個標籤:`<KEY>文字</KEY>`,全部在 `<dict>...</dict>` 內。\\n3. xlsx「多國語言」工作表:**列 = Key、欄 = 語系**,語系欄標題的括號內就是代碼,\\n   例如「繁體中文(tw)zh-TW」→ `tw`。\\n\\n驗證一次:`ls assets/game/Text` 看是否一堆語系目錄、各有 `gameStrings.xml`;\\n打開一個確認是 `<KEY>文字</KEY>` 格式。不符合(例如商用部門用別種格式)→ **不要用此 skill**,改手動或另議。\\n\\n## 工作流\\n\\n### 1. 檢視 xlsx,確認語系欄與列號\\n\\n用 openpyxl 看「多國語言」工作表的第 1 列(欄標題)與要處理的資料列:\\n\\n```python\\nimport openpyxl\\nwb = openpyxl.load_workbook(\\\"規格.xlsx\\\", read_only=True, data_only=True)\\nws = wb[[s for s in wb.sheetnames if \\\"多國語言\\\" in s][0]]\\nfor i, row in enumerate(ws.iter_rows(min_row=1, max_row=60, values_only=True), 1):\\n    print(i, [ (c or \\\"\\\")[:20] if isinstance(c, str) else c for c in row[:5] ])\\n```\\n\\n### 2. 建對應表(由你 agent 建立,不是叫使用者手填)\\n\\n`string_keys.tsv` 是中繼產物,由 **你(agent)** 依使用者給的資訊建立/填寫。\\n使用者通常只會「動嘴」:說「把這幾個 Key 加進去」或「Key=X 在第 N 列」。你的工作:\\n\\n- 使用者給了 Key + 列號 → 直接落成 tsv 各一行。\\n- 使用者只給 Key 清單、不知道列號 → 你去 xlsx 比對文字幫他定位列號,再填。\\n- 只有單筆 → 臨時建一行的 tsv 也行(例如 `_claude_temp/string_keys.tsv`)。\\n\\n複製 `scripts/string_keys.tsv` 範本當起點,每行 `Key <Tab> 列號 <Tab> 備註`。\\n\\n🔴 **列號 = xlsx 左側的「實際 Excel 列號」,不是內部「編號」欄。** 編號欄常中途斷掉,拿來當識別會錯位。\\n\\n### 3. dry-run 預覽 + 語意核對(最重要)\\n\\n```bash\\npython <此skill>/scripts/sync_game_strings.py --xlsx 規格.xlsx --mapping _claude_temp/string_keys.tsv\\n```\\n\\ndry-run 只印不寫。**務必核對每個 Key 對到的列,語意是否正確** —— 把該列的 en/tw 文字\\n跟現有 XML 裡同 Key 的值比一比。實務上「列號填錯一格」很常見且 dry-run 數字看起來照樣正常\\n(例如 EXTRA_BET_DESC 本該對「OFF→ON 開啟選擇畫面」那列,卻填到「初始乘倍」那列),\\n唯一抓得到的方法就是人眼核對語意。報告會列出每語系「新增/更新/缺字」,缺翻譯的語系要回報補齊。\\n\\n### 4. 確認無誤才寫入\\n\\n```bash\\npython <此skill>/scripts/sync_game_strings.py --xlsx 規格.xlsx --mapping _claude_temp/string_keys.tsv --write\\n```\\n\\n行為:Key 已存在 → 覆蓋更新;不存在 → 插在 `</dict>` 前。保留各檔原本換行(CRLF/LF)與 UTF-8,\\n文字自動做 XML 跳脫(`& < >`)。寫入後用 `git diff` 抽查 2-3 個語系確認內容正確。\\n\\n## 腳本參數\\n\\n`scripts/sync_game_strings.py`(路徑全相對「當前工作目錄」,在專案根目錄執行):\\n\\n| 參數 | 預設 | 說明 |\\n|------|------|------|\\n| `--xlsx` | (必填) | 規格 xlsx 路徑 |\\n| `--mapping` | `./string_keys.tsv` | Key↔列號對應表 |\\n| `--text-dir` | `./assets/game/Text` | 語系目錄根 |\\n| `--sheet` | 自動偵測含「多國語言」者 | 工作表名(多個符合時需手動指定) |\\n| `--xml-name` | `gameStrings.xml` | 各語系內 XML 檔名 |\\n| `--write` | (關;預設 dry-run) | 加上才實際寫入 |\\n\\n需要 `openpyxl`(`pip install openpyxl`)。\\n\\n## 常見問題\\n\\n- **某語系報「找不到對應 gameStrings.xml」**:欄標題括號代碼與目錄名對不上 → 檢查 xlsx 標題或目錄。\\n- **缺字(∅)**:該列在某語系儲存格是空的 → 翻譯未提供,回報請對方補,腳本不會寫空字串。\\n- **git 跳 `CRLF will be replaced by LF`**:repo 換行設定行為,非腳本造成,磁碟檔仍為原換行。\\n- **新 Key 大量新增後**順序在 `</dict>` 前,屬正常;不影響讀取。\\n\",\"numLines\":97,\"startLine\":1,\"totalLines\":97}}"</outcome>
</observed_from_primary_session>

content.thinking:
The user read the SKILL.md file for uk-slot-multilang-sync. This is a discovery - they're examining what the existing skill does to evaluate whether integration is needed (as mentioned in the original user request "檢視ok了，幫我判斷是否需要整合至Skill").

The skill is for syncing multilingual text from xlsx spec files into gameStrings.xml files across multiple locales for UK online slot machine framework projects. Key details:
- Works with UK framework convention (plist format gameStrings.xml, one directory per locale)
- Syncs from xlsx "多國語言" worksheet (rows = keys, columns = locales)
- Uses a mapping TSV file (string_keys.tsv) to map keys to row numbers
- Has dry-run and write modes
- Script located at scripts/sync_game_strings.py

This is a discovery observation about the existing skill's functionality.

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>UK Slot Multilang-Sync Skill Functionality Review</title>
  <subtitle>Skill automates batch syncing of multilingual strings from xlsx specs into gameStrings.xml across 26 locales</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>uk-slot-multilang-sync skill syncs text from xlsx "多國語言" worksheet to assets/game/Text/{locale}/gameStrings.xml</fact>
    <fact>Skill requires UK framework convention: plist-format gameStrings.xml with KEY-value tags in dict structure</fact>
    <fact>Script sync_game_strings.py uses string_keys.tsv mapping file (Key, row number, notes) to locate xlsx data</fact>
    <fact>Workflow includes dry-run preview for semantic validation before --write flag commits changes</fact>
    <fact>Row numbers must be actual Excel row numbers not internal numbering column to avoid misalignment</fact>
    <fact>Agent (not user) is responsible for creating/populating string_keys.tsv mapping based on user requirements</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Reviewed the complete uk-slot-multilang-sync skill documentation to evaluate integration needs. The skill automates multilingual text synchronization for UK online slot machine projects by batch-updating gameStrings.xml files across all locales from xlsx specifications. The workflow involves: (1) inspecting xlsx structure with openpyxl to identify locale columns and data rows, (2) agent-generated mapping TSV linking Keys to xlsx row numbers, (3) dry-run preview with semantic validation to catch misaligned mappings, and (4) write operation with XML escaping and original file format preservation. The skill emphasizes that the agent must build the mapping file based on user descriptions rather than requiring manual user input, and stresses the critical importance of semantic validation during dry-run since row number misalignment is a common error that numeric checks alone cannot catch.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>pattern</concept>
    <concept>gotcha</concept>
  </concepts>

    <file>C:\Users\jiunchiwang\.claude\skills\uk-slot-multilang-sync\SKILL.md</file>

</observation>

<observed_from_primary_session>
  <what_happened>Write</what_happened>
  <occurred_at>2026-06-16T05:36:31.999Z</occurred_at>
  <working_directory>G:\Cocos_Project\uk_pirates_queen\assets\game\Text\logo</working_directory>
  <parameters>"{\"file_path\":\"C:\\\\Users\\\\jiunchiwang\\\\.claude\\\\projects\\\\G--Cocos-Project-uk-pirates-queen\\\\memory\\\\cocos-per-locale-asset-copy.md\",\"content\":\"---\\nname: cocos-per-locale-asset-copy\\ndescription: Cocos 多語系圖片資源複製：只複製 .png 不複製 .meta，避免 UUID 衝突；meta 由編輯器 import 自動生成\\nmetadata:\\n  type: project\\n---\\n\\n在本專案要把某 base 語系的圖片資源複製成全 26 語系（如 `logo_en.png` → `logo_<語系>.png`，放在 `assets/game/Text/<資源>/`）時：\\n\\n**只複製 `.png`，絕不複製 `.meta`。**\\n\\n**Why**：Cocos 的 `.meta` 內含唯一 UUID（例：`logo_en.png.meta` 的 `77b4a4f1-...`）。若連 `.meta` 一起複製，25 個檔會共用同一 UUID → asset-db UUID 衝突，會壞。\\n\\n**How to apply**：\\n1. 只複製 png：`for loc in <25語系>; do cp logo_en.png \\\"logo_${loc}.png\\\"; done`（語系清單見 [[multilang-26-locales]]，排除 base 語系）\\n2. 不動 `.meta`。新 png 由 Cocos Creator 開啟/reimport 時自動生成各自獨立的 meta+UUID。\\n3. 編輯器沒開時複製照樣成立，下次開編輯器會自動 import；可選用 `mcp__cocos362__asset_reimport` 逐一確認。\\n\\n注意：複製出來的圖內容都跟 base 一樣，只是建好「檔名+位置」佔位；各語系正式 logo 美術圖仍需後續逐一替換。\\n\\n文字類多語系（gameStrings.xml）走 `uk-slot-multilang-sync` skill，與此圖片流程不同機制。\\n\"}"</parameters>
  <outcome>"{\"type\":\"create\",\"filePath\":\"C:\\\\Users\\\\jiunchiwang\\\\.claude\\\\projects\\\\G--Cocos-Project-uk-pirates-queen\\\\memory\\\\cocos-per-locale-asset-copy.md\",\"content\":\"---\\nname: cocos-per-locale-asset-copy\\ndescription: Cocos 多語系圖片資源複製：只複製 .png 不複製 .meta，避免 UUID 衝突；meta 由編輯器 import 自動生成\\nmetadata: \\n  node_type: memory\\n  type: project\\n  originSessionId: 6e31ccd0-84e4-47f3-bf68-6d31ab26369f\\n---\\n\\n在本專案要把某 base 語系的圖片資源複製成全 26 語系（如 `logo_en.png` → `logo_<語系>.png`，放在 `assets/game/Text/<資源>/`）時：\\n\\n**只複製 `.png`，絕不複製 `.meta`。**\\n\\n**Why**：Cocos 的 `.meta` 內含唯一 UUID（例：`logo_en.png.meta` 的 `77b4a4f1-...`）。若連 `.meta` 一起複製，25 個檔會共用同一 UUID → asset-db UUID 衝突，會壞。\\n\\n**How to apply**：\\n1. 只複製 png：`for loc in <25語系>; do cp logo_en.png \\\"logo_${loc}.png\\\"; done`（語系清單見 [[multilang-26-locales]]，排除 base 語系）\\n2. 不動 `.meta`。新 png 由 Cocos Creator 開啟/reimport 時自動生成各自獨立的 meta+UUID。\\n3. 編輯器沒開時複製照樣成立，下次開編輯器會自動 import；可選用 `mcp__cocos362__asset_reimport` 逐一確認。\\n\\n注意：複製出來的圖內容都跟 base 一樣，只是建好「檔名+位置」佔位；各語系正式 logo 美術圖仍需後續逐一替換。\\n\\n文字類多語系（gameStrings.xml）走 `uk-slot-multilang-sync` skill，與此圖片流程不同機制。\\n\",\"structuredPatch\":[],\"originalFile\":null,\"userModified\":false}"</outcome>
</observed_from_primary_session>

content.thinking:
The user created a memory file documenting a pattern for copying multilingual image assets in Cocos Creator projects. This is important knowledge about avoiding UUID conflicts when duplicating .png files across 26 locales.

Key points:
- Only copy .png files, never .meta files
- .meta files contain unique UUIDs that would conflict if duplicated
- Cocos Creator auto-generates .meta files on import
- This is for image assets, separate from the text-based multilang-sync skill
- 26 locales are involved (referenced from multilang-26-locales)

This is a "change" type since it's documenting a practice/pattern, but could also be "decision" since it documents a choice with rationale. I think "decision" fits better because it explains WHY (UUID conflicts) and the trade-offs.

Actually, looking more carefully - this is creating project memory documentation. It's recording knowledge/a pattern for the project. This seems more like a "decision" about how to handle this workflow, with clear rationale.

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>decision</type>
  <title>Cocos Per-Locale Asset Copy Pattern Documented</title>
  <subtitle>Only copy PNG files without meta files to avoid UUID conflicts across 26 locales</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Project memory document cocos-per-locale-asset-copy.md created at C:\Users\jiunchiwang\.claude\projects\G--Cocos-Project-uk-pirates-queen\memory\</fact>
    <fact>Pattern requires copying only .png files when creating per-locale image assets, never copying .meta files</fact>
    <fact>Cocos Creator .meta files contain unique UUIDs that cause asset-db conflicts if duplicated across files</fact>
    <fact>Cocos Creator auto-generates .meta files with independent UUIDs during import or editor open</fact>
    <fact>Workflow applies to 26 locales for assets in assets/game/Text/ directories</fact>
    <fact>Image asset copying is separate mechanism from text-based uk-slot-multilang-sync skill for gameStrings.xml</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Created project memory documenting the proper workflow for duplicating image assets across multiple locales in Cocos Creator projects. The pattern establishes that only .png files should be copied (e.g., logo_en.png → logo_{locale}.png) while .meta files must never be duplicated. This prevents UUID conflicts in Cocos Creator's asset database, as each .meta file contains a unique UUID identifier. The editor automatically generates independent .meta files with unique UUIDs when importing new .png files. Initial copies use base locale content as placeholders, with actual locale-specific artwork replaced later. This image asset workflow is distinct from the text-based multilang synchronization handled by the uk-slot-multilang-sync skill.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>why-it-exists</concept>
    <concept>gotcha</concept>
    <concept>pattern</concept>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
  </concepts>
